title: lathyrus sylvestris proles pyrenaicus
intro: Lathyrus sylvestris proles pyrenaicus, also known as the Pyrenean vetchling, is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the vetchling plant. This perennial legume is native to the Pyrenees mountain range and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains a compound called ODAP (oxalydiamino propionic acid), which has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation. The Pyrenean vetchling can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to the potential toxicity of ODAP.
